sourceList.title: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem van Wijffliet (born n.n. van Wijffliet)

zh.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet於大約1230年出生在荷蘭。她和Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet至少有2個兒子。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet于大约1230年出生在荷兰。她和Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet至少有2个儿子。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: born
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Heinz, Helmut, Berthold, Frieda, Friedrich, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst, Jutta.
English: variant of   Bourne  .
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French borgne ‘one-eyed, squint-eyed’.

variants.name: Bourne
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Borner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerd, Hans, Irmgard.
North German (also Börner): variant of Born , + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
North German: occupational name for a stockman, from Middle Low German bornen ‘to water (farm animals)’.

nameMeanings.name: n.n.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written នួន, same as   Nuon  .
English: variant of   Nunn  .

variants.name: Nuon
meanings: Cambodian: written នួន, from a Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘(to be) fine, wellbehaved’, also (of girls) ‘sweet’. Compare   Noun   and   Nun  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: N.n.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a pet form of Ann (for the initial N-, compare Ned ). It is now also widely used as a short form of Nancy .

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet was born about 1230, in Netherlands. She had at least 2 sons with Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet est née environ 1230, à Pays-Bas. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet wurde um 1230 in Niederlande geboren. Sie hatte mit Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet è nata il circa 1230 nel Paesi Bassi. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijfflietは約1230年にオランダで生まれた。彼女はJan Willem Simon van Wijfflietとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet(은)는 대략 1230년 네덜란드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet nasceu aproximadamente 1230, em Países Baixos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet родилась около 1230 г. в местности Нидерланды. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: N.n. Vrouw Jan Willem born n.n. van Wijffliet nació aproximadamente 1230, en Países Bajos. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Jan Willem Simon van Wijffliet. Murió en su pueblo.
